message:
fix(routes): fix boolean query param parsing in memory and cass routes

z.coerce.boolean() uses JavaScript's Boolean() function which treats any
non-empty string as truthy, causing "false" to incorrectly become true.
This could lead to unintended behavior like auto-fixes being applied when
?fix=false is passed.

Fixed by using a proper transform that explicitly handles "true"/"false"
and "1"/"0" string values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
